About

Hi! I'm a CS/engineering student with interests that range from machine learning research to web security to building things that feel good to use.

Right now I'm working on adapTrunc, a project exploring whether we can use class separability as a proxy to find the optimal truncation depth in BERT-style models — the goal being faster inference without fine-tuning every layer.

Outside of research, I spend time on security labs, building small web tools, and occasionally writing about what I'm learning here on the blog.
